what_it_does: Defines a React TypeBuilder surface for creating, editing, saving, deleting, previewing, and layout-switching Type drafts against window.CV_REGISTRY, with slot and variable editing helpers and optional Vi-assisted proposal/suggestion flows.
observations: Uses aliased React hooks pulled from the global React object.
Keeps draft Type state local and reinitializes it when initialTypeId changes.
Supports split, wizard, and canvas layouts through local layout state and layout-specific components.
Supports visual, form, vi, and json modes in the header controls, passing the mode through to child surfaces.
Uses window.CV_REGISTRY for lookups, querying compatible parent candidates, registering saved Types, and removing Types.
Uses window.CV_TYPES_VI for async Type proposal and slot suggestion flows.
Slot and variable mutation helpers update nested draft structures immutably at shallow levels.
Saving forks built-in registry entries into user provenance after confirmation.
The file defines the container, draft lifecycle, and three layout wrappers while delegating detailed library, preview, inspector, form, slot, variable, and review views to referenced components.
standouts: Centralizes draft lifecycle and registry persistence for Type authoring.
Combines manual editing, layout switching, and Vi-assisted generation in one authoring shell.
Treats editing a built-in Type as a fork into user provenance.
conventions: Uses functional React components with local hook state.
Uses global browser objects for application services and UI feedback.
Uses shallow object spreading for state updates.
Uses JSX conditional rendering for mode, layout, and modal state.
Uses dsa-* and tb-* class naming for buttons, panes, wizard, and canvas regions.
Exports by assigning TypeBuilder onto window rather than using module exports.
concerns: Several runtime dependencies are read from window without local guards beyond limited optional chaining.
viSuggestSlots has no catch branch, so rejected suggestions may clear busy without user-facing error feedback.
The JSON mode is selectable but no JSON-specific renderer is defined in this file.
patchSlot cannot rename a slot key; it only updates the object stored under an existing key.
addSlot derives a slot name from current count, which can collide after deletions or existing non-sequential slot names.
removeType calls R.remove(draft.id) without checking that draft.id is present or registered.
notes: This file is the Type authoring shell: it owns draft state, registry save/delete behavior, Vi requests, and layout routing.
questions: What exact schema does CV_REGISTRY.register expect for slots, defaults, variables, tags, and provenance?
How should json mode be rendered by the delegated preview or inspector components?
Should unsaved changes be detected or warned on close, since sourceRef tracks the source but no dirty check is present?
What guarantees exist around CV_TYPES_VI availability and returned proposal shape?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it provides a universal Type author/edit shell with multiple layouts and modes, while detailed editors and previews are delegated to referenced child components.
apparent_themes: Forking built-in definitions into user space
Multiple authoring modes over the same draft model
intention_signals: The file seems intended to make Type creation and editing approachable through several UI layouts while keeping a single draft model that can be manually edited, Vi-proposed, reviewed, and persisted into a registry.
novelty: A registry Type builder that combines visual/form/json mode selection, three interchangeable layouts, and Vi-assisted schema drafting within a single global-runtime React file.
contribution: Contributes a file-local authoring controller for Type substrates: draft initialization, slot and variable mutation, registry persistence, built-in forking behavior, layer-compatible parent selection, Vi-assisted proposal hooks, and split/wizard/canvas layout orchestration.
summary_for_embedding: React file defining a TypeBuilder authoring shell for registry-backed Type drafts with slot and variable editing, save/delete/reset behavior, Vi proposal support, and split/wizard/canvas layouts.
intention_for_embedding: Built to let users create or edit design-system Types at any layer through multiple authoring modes while preserving registry persistence and optional Vi assistance.

symbols.does: Renders and manages the Type Builder container for authoring or editing a registry Type.
symbols.description: Owns layout, mode, draft, filter, busy, brief, and slot-browser state. It wires draft mutation helpers, Vi proposal helpers, save/reset/delete behavior, parent candidate calculation, header actions, layout selection, and the slot browser modal into one Type authoring surface.

symbols.does: Applies a shallow partial update to the draft Type object.
symbols.description: This helper merges an incoming object into the current draft via setDraft. It is used as the general field patching primitive for top-level Type fields.

symbols.does: Applies a shallow partial update to a named slot in draft.slots.
symbols.description: It preserves existing slots and the existing slot object for the target name, then merges the provided slot patch into that entry. The helper can create a slot object under a given key if it does not already exist.

symbols.does: Deletes a named slot from the draft Type.
symbols.description: It clones the current slots object, deletes the requested key, and stores the resulting slots object back on the draft. The deletion is local to draft state until saved.

symbols.does: Adds a default block-accepting slot to the draft.
symbols.description: It creates a slot key from the current number of draft slots and inserts a default slot labeled New slot. The default slot accepts block-layer content, is not multiple, and is not optional.

symbols.does: Applies a shallow partial update to one variable by array index.
symbols.description: It maps over draft.variables and merges the provided patch into the variable at the matching index. Other variables are preserved unchanged.

symbols.does: Removes one variable from the draft by array index.
symbols.description: It filters draft.variables to exclude the selected index and writes the filtered array back to draft state. This only changes local draft state until the user saves.

symbols.does: Appends a default text variable to the draft.
symbols.description: It creates a new variable with a generated var_N key, New variable label, empty default value, and text kind. Existing variables are preserved and the new variable is appended.

symbols.does: Requests a Vi-generated Type proposal from the current brief and draft hints.
symbols.description: It requires a non-empty brief, sets busy state, calls window.CV_TYPES_VI.proposeType with parent, layer, and family hints, then replaces the draft with the proposal while preserving a user-entered non-new id. It reports success or failure through dsaToast.

symbols.does: Requests Vi slot suggestions for a saved draft Type and merges new slot names into the draft.
symbols.description: It requires the draft id to exist in the registry, calls window.CV_TYPES_VI.suggestSlots, and ignores suggested slots whose names already exist. New suggestions are normalized into label, accepts, multiple, and optional fields before being added.

symbols.does: Validates and registers the draft Type in the registry.
symbols.description: It requires a name and stable id, checks whether an existing built-in Type is being edited, and asks for confirmation before forking it into user provenance. It registers the draft through R.register, reports the saved name, and calls onSaved with the registered Type.

symbols.does: Reverts the draft to the current source Type or a new default draft.
symbols.description: It re-runs initializeDraft using sourceRef.current and the registry. It also reports the revert through dsaToast.

symbols.does: Confirms and deletes the current draft Type from the registry.
symbols.description: It prompts with the draft name, calls R.remove with the draft id, reports deletion, and invokes onClose. The function assumes the current draft id is the Type to remove.

symbols.does: Creates the initial draft object for an existing Type or a new Type.
symbols.description: If an id is supplied and found in the registry, it returns a deep JSON clone of that Type. Otherwise it returns a new user-provenance block Type draft with generated new.* id, empty name and description, general family, no lineage, empty slots/defaults/variables/tags, and a check-square icon.

symbols.does: Renders the split layout with library, preview, and inspector panes.
symbols.description: It places BuilderLibrary in the left aside, BuilderPreview in the center, and BuilderInspector in the right aside. The full props object is passed through so the delegated components can read and mutate the shared draft state.

symbols.does: Renders a step-based Type authoring workflow.
symbols.description: It keeps local step state and defines six steps: Basics, Lineage, Vi, Slots, Vars, and Review. Depending on the active step it renders the matching form/editor/review component and exposes Back and Continue navigation.

symbols.does: Renders a large canvas preview with a toggleable floating inspector.
symbols.description: It keeps local inspector-open state, renders BuilderPreview with the big flag, and provides a handle that toggles the inspector. When open, it renders BuilderInspector in an embedded canvas inspector aside.
